Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses it 183 times in a leap year (since there are 366 days). To calculate the total amount of shampoo she needs, we multiply the amount used per wash by the number of washes: $ \frac{1}{4} \times 183 = 45.75 $ ounces. Since each shampoo bottle contains 14 ounces, we divide the total amount by the bottle size: $ \frac{45.75}{14} \approx 3.27 $. Therefore, Brittany needs [number of bottles] to get through a leap year. She should round up to ensure she has enough shampoo, so she will need [rounded number of bottles].

Each shampoo bottle provides 14 ounces, and with 3.27 bottles required, Brittany must purchase 4 bottles to cover her needs. This ensures she has enough shampoo for all her washes throughout the year. It's important to note that [number of bottles] represents the exact calculation, while [rounded number of bottles] reflects the practical choice of buying whole bottles. By following this approach, Brittany can maintain her hair washing routine without running out of shampoo.
